(00:01)
All right, so we’re going to do another lead example. I think for this one, I’m
going to go ahead and pull up a CBD offer. This one did really well. I wrote
it and then it ended up causing a bit of trouble because it was frankly a little
ahead of its time. It was before CBD became more popular and accepted and
it ran into some banking issues with the people who were doing the merchant
processing for it that then led to a pretty unfortunate situation. But that being
said, the conversions on this thing were extremely good and at one point this
was doing - well, let me not talk about, sales figures on video, but it was doing
pretty well. So it’s for a CBD offer and honestly, I don’t think this lead is like
that special, but there’s some cool stuff about it and I’ll explain it.
(01:03)
So I’m going to read it and then we’ll go back and we’ll match it with the
outline again. It goes, “Ivy League scientists have called it one of the most
powerful pain relievers on the planet” - already a typo, let me start over. “Ivy
League scientists have called it one of the most powerful pain relievers on
the planet. Doctors are having an astounding success using it for patients
who have “untreatable pain.” They’re giving it to the folks whom they’d given
up hope on and they’re shocked to see that after their patients use this, their
suffering and aches begin getting better fast and in some cases, overnight.
This pain reliever is also completely non-habit forming, has no side effects,
is 100% natural and starts working in as little as 30 minutes and it’s now
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

finally legal in the United States for the first time since 1937, Which is the year
that three of the richest Americans in history spent millions of dollars in an

It’s not marijuana, but it is something top scientists are calling the biggest

been featured extensively on Dr. Oz, NBC, CNN, The Today Show, ESPN and
dozens of other major news sources and today, tens of thousands of people
say it’s helped with their nerve pain, joint pain, back pain, arthritis and more.
Former NFL and MLB stars, army veterans and Dr. Sanjay Gupta of CNN say
it can make your discomfort and soreness seem to just “disappear” and this
natural breakthrough has been shown to aid with numerous other conditions
and health challenges as well.
(02:31)
In fact, when I’m about to share with you is so effective that the US government
has actually patented it. Patent number 6,630,507 and inside the patent, which
was done by the Department of Health and Human Services, the government
states that this incredible pain relieving secret can act as an antiepileptic and
antiseizure, is a powerful antioxidant, can assist in the aftermath of a stroke
along with other traumatic injuries, can help support healthy vision, promotes
healthy cells and so much more.
(02:58)
Now I’m going to tell you what this natural elixir for pain relief is right inside
this short video and I’ll also show you all the science behind how it works while
sharing the specific clinical and double-blind studies that back everything I’m
saying up. But before I do that, I do want to warn you, the drug companies
who control American medicine do not want you to know about this. They’re
afraid that because this proven pain-relieving discovery is so powerful yet
safe, natural, non-habit forming and dramatically inexpensive that if this
breakthrough goes mainstream, it will cost them billions in lost profits.
(03:36)
As 	a 	result 	of 	all 	of 	this, 	I 	can’t 	guarantee 	how 	long 	the 	solution 	will
remain available to the public or that it won’t end up getting banned by big

MODULE 7	Lead Example - CBD
presentation in its entirety because while the solution can provide many with
nearly immediate relief from the agonizing pain they’ve been suffering from
for years, it may not be available for long. Okay, so let me give you all the
details right now.
(03:59)
Hi, my name is Adam” and it goes into here and tell his story, which the story
really helps complement the lead a little bit, but I think this is enough to get
you through it.
(04:08)
Coming back to the outline. Well, calling out the problem: untreatable pain,
pain reliever, pain people giving up hope on - sorry, I cut myself off there - all
that stuff. Promising solution, again: powerful pain reliever, untreatable pain.
I mean they work in tandem here. Emotional discovery story: no, but it does
go into an emotional story right after the lead with the story of this guy named
Aaron. I guess, one thing that’s unique about this one is that was the lead I
read to you and then it keeps going. In fact, I’ll give you the rest of it.
(04:54)
“Hi, my name is Adam. I’m a pastor who lives with my wife and four beautiful
children and the medicinal plant I’m talking about is hemp. Now you might
be thinking it’s crazy for a pastor and family man would be telling everyone
he knows to start using a plant that’s the cousin of marijuana, but the truth is
hemp is one of the most health-rich plants in all of God’s green earth given to us
by nature to help with pain and dozens of other ailments. It’s also legal in all 50
states and it’s impossible to get “high” off of hemp because it doesn’t contain
THC, which is the psychoactive chemical in “pot” that gets people “stoned.”
(05:23)
More importantly is the fact that scientists have now isolated the single most
powerful natural compound inside of hemp and folks who use this special
compound are seeing even their worst cases of pain start to fade away in
as little as 30 minutes. In fact, that’s what happened to my one of my close
friends, Aaron, and want to quickly share his story with you.
(05:39)
Back in the 90s, Aaron was a Division 1 college quarterback during which
time he had some amazing games, but he also took one heck of a beating.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

6	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 7	Lead Example - CBD
The physical punishment continued after Aaron graduated, too, as he made
his way out to numerous NFL practice squads and also spent time playing in
the Arena football league. By the time Aaron retired from football in 2005, his
body was in shambles.
(05:58)
Aaron suffered from shoulder injuries, a bad back, destroyed knees and swollen
wrists. Plus, Aaron’s hips were so bad that he had to get hip replacement
surgery. What really made Aaron scared, though was the fact that he was only
in his 30s. If things were this bad now, what would they be like in his 50s, 60s,
70s and beyond? To make matters worse, Aaron was using painkillers on a
daily basis, which were wrecking his liver and making him go through his days
feeling like a drugged down zombie.
(06:22)
Maybe you can relate to some of this. Perhaps it hurts every time you bend
over to pick up the paper. Maybe you can’t play tennis or the piano or swing a
golf club anymore without feeling terrible pain or maybe you’re one of those
people who is dependent on painkillers to get by every day. If any of those
things describes you or someone that you know, then this next part will really
excite you.
(06:39)
You see, Aaron was miserable and he thought his life was ruined, but then
Aaron discovered the all-natural hemp extract I’ll be sharing inside this
presentation and suddenly, almost overnight, Aaron’s pain was gone. Within
days, Aaron says he felt 15 years younger. He had his youthful speed and
agility back and today, Aaron even plays competitive beach volleyball where
he’s frequently seen dropping to one knee and jumping back up, diving and
leaping and running around for hours at a time.
(07:01)
Aaron does all this about any of the constant arm, hip and back pain he’d
suffered from for over a decade and if this hemp extract can work for a former
NCAA Division 1 quarterback like him, you better bet it can work for you, too.
Of course, what you’re probably asking is, ‘Well yeah, but how does it work?’
So let me go ahead and answer that question for you right now.”
(07:17)
Honestly, I guess really that’s the background story because then after that

7	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 7	Lead Example - CBD
in the sales letter, it does go into the mechanism, but so yeah, I don’t know,
I guess you just got the bonus copy there for this one, but back to what you
were talking about, calls out the problem, calls out the pain point doesn’t
really tease the emotional discovery story and probably could have done that
in here somewhere maybe talking about Aaron’s story. Contrarian nature
of the mechanism, for sure. Just the fact that it’s like banned, but it’s not
marijuana. Fascinations, not in a pure classical sense, but the whole idea of
it being patented by the government is totally a little hooker fascination that
you can have in there and again, the banned since 1937 is a total fascination
or curiosity thing. Credibility builders, well, the US patent for sure, Dr. Sanjay
Gupta, former NFL and MLB stars, army veterans, a lot of that. Qualifiers,
it works for everybody. Talks about well, the idea of if it works for all these
people it can work for you so that’s a qualifier and then we don’t have any
testimonials on this one.
(08:26)
But beyond the outline, why did this work so well? Part of it was just being
ahead of its time, but it’s strong promises and at the time, I mean, really, CBD
has pretty amazing benefits for people if it’s the right CBD. One thing I think is
cool that I did here again, I like the idea “it’s legal for the first time since 1937
and which is a year that three of the richest Americans in history spent millions
of dollars in an attempt to ban this blessing from everyday citizens like you
and me. More than that in a minute.” I mean I like that thing but then I thought,
because if people are thinking back like oh, he’s talking about marijuana
especially at the time where CBD wasn’t as popular when this was written.
(09:11)
So I love that line right there “it’s not marijuana, but it’s something top
scientists are calling the biggest breakthrough in pain relief since the discovery
of aspirin.” I think that’s important because like I said, the thing is, if you’re
teasing something super obvious and you have to acknowledge that it’s not
that thing or if it is that thing you have a problem or you have to explain why
it’s not what you think. You can’t be like, it can help with your immune system
and boosts antioxidants and people like to have it in juice, blah blah blah. It
comes from orange juice, people are like, “Oh yeah, it’s vitamin C. Cool, I can
go buy at the store.” So you’d be like, “But it’s not vitamin C” or if it is vitamin
C, you’d be like - this is a totally different example but the same principle -
you’d be like, “And yes, it has to do with vitamin C but not the kind you can
buy at the store and I’ll explain why in a minute.” So that’s super important
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 9 --

8	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 7	Lead Example - CBD
because if it’s obvious then people are going to be like, “Oh, I know what this
is,” and stop watching or reading.
(10:07)
But the patent thing is a great curiosity, I think that’s really good to have it in
there. The idea of people wouldn’t want you to know about this. I mean it’s
always given, you can have that, it gets a little overdone but frankly, Big Pharma
doesn’t like CBD and especially at the time, there was a lot of resistance to it
including the lobbyists so I felt fine putting all of that in there.
(10:31)
So is there anything else I should add to this? I made a lot of promises.
You’ll notice the big focus is on pain, but we do have other benefits from that
patent like antiepileptic and anti-seizure, a powerful antioxidant, can assist
in the aftermath of a stroke - again, we’re not saying it’s going to prevent a
stroke; we’re just saying the aftermath of a stroke, it can make things better
- can help support healthy vision, promotes healthy cells. We’re adding some
additional benefits that maybe people don’t care as much about pain, they’ll
still want to keep watching or reading or whatever. More credibility: the
specific clinical and double-blind studies, too. That’s important because you
need to have one big focus, one big problem that you’re solving in the lead,
but if it helps some other stuff, you can tease that stuff, too. You just can’t
try to be everything to everybody.
(11:24)
Hopefully, that makes sense. So another one here and we’ll keep going. We’ll
do a couple more.
